q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is eval(expr, {...}, {...}) on line 97; reasoning states it leads to arbitrary code execution via attribute access on locals

mock_guidance.target: Mock or record the built-in eval() call on line 97
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the first argument 'expr' passed to eval to verify it contains user-controlled input from request.GET.get('expr', ...)
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a safe default value such as 49 (42 + 7, the result of the default expression 'amount + fee') to allow execution to continue without executing the tainted string
notes: The eval call restricts __builtins__ but still allows dangerous attribute traversal on the locals, so it must not be invoked with untrusted input.
If the harness can import the function, patching builtins.eval via unittest.mock.patch('builtins.eval') is the cleanest approach.
